reviews.text:
Life-Changing Experience – Exceptional Legal Representation
I am profoundly grateful to share that my asylum application has been officially approved by the USCIS (Immigration Office). This life-changing milestone would not have been possible without the exceptional legal representation provided by the Law Office of Keshab Raj Seadie (LOKRS) and the dedicated efforts of Mr. Ram Dhoj Bista.
From the very first consultation through to the final approval, both Mr. Keshab Raj Seadie and Mr. Bista demonstrated extraordinary professionalism, unwavering dedication, and deep compassion. Despite the complexity and emotional weight of my case, Mr. Seadie took it on with confidence and entrusted Mr. Bista with full responsibility for its management. Mr. Bista approached every detail with meticulous care, carefully reviewed all documentation, guided me through each step, and ensured I was thoroughly prepared for the interview and submission of evidence.
What truly sets Mr. Seadie and Mr. Bista apart is not only their legal expertise and decades of experience but their genuine commitment to the individuals they serve. They did not treat me merely as a client, but as a human being whose safety and future truly mattered. Mr. Bista’s steady encouragement, empathy, and patience during the most trying moments of this journey gave me strength and hope when I needed it most.
The firm’s staff were equally professional—always responsive, respectful, and efficient. The office environment was consistently welcoming and supportive, which significantly alleviated the stress associated with the process.
Today, I stand in a place of peace and security, and I owe it to this exceptional legal team. For anyone seeking trustworthy, compassionate, and result-driven immigration attorneys, I wholeheartedly recommend Mr. Keshab Raj Seadie and his remarkable team.
Thank you for helping me begin a new chapter of my life with dignity and hope.
— Prakash Baral

If you want a solid team to fight for you, Johnson & Associates is who I recommend.  Chris, Michelle, Morgan and the team worked tirelessly to achieve my goals in a very hostile business dispute.  Hon. Michelle Slaughter (former Judge) went above and beyond. I’m so glad a found someone with core Christian values to look out for my family. I highly recommend this firm as long as you’re willing to cooperate diligently with any documentation they request.

Chris and Morgan are fabulous.

Update: I reached out to Chris Johnson about Brandon Evans, he said he would get back to me as soon as possible & never did. I ended up going with another law firm & received amazing results, the law firm I should’ve hired the first time instead of wasting my time & money with Johnson & Associates. If you need a GREAT firm who can produce actual results I recommend Tough Law Firm, not Johnson & Associates. I hired Brandon Evans and the outcome of hiring him is the same outcome I would have received without hiring a lawyer. I have reoccurring mold and have documented it and spoke to the landlord about it multiple times with it coming back. I hired a lawyer so the mold issue could be actually taken care of but instead Brandon produced the same results I have received time after time. Not only that he told me he would speak to the landlord about us getting reimbursed for the mold testing. When I have asked him about the reimbursement he hasn’t replied to any of my emails, I have sent FIVE with no reply, and these emails are weeks apart.

Communication has been terrible with Brandon. Not only that I was sent an invoice for things I shouldn’t have been billed for. When I questioned him about the invoice he said to not pay it I shouldn’t have been billed. I know I will need a lawyer when the mold comes back but this situation has left a bad taste in my mouth. I don’t want to spend more money to get the same results that I would’ve gotten if I didn’t hire a lawyer. I have already reached out to another law firm that I have hired before for a different situation and I am confident they will handle my case and produce the results I originally paid for.

reviews.text:
We cannot say enough good things about David. After a deeply frustrating and stressful experience with a previous attorney, we were anxious and honestly a bit defeated when we found ourselves needing new counsel mid-adoption. From the moment David stepped in, everything changed.

David took over our case with professionalism, urgency, and genuine care. He immediately got up to speed, clearly explained where things stood, and laid out a concrete plan to move us forward. What felt overwhelming suddenly felt manageable. His communication was prompt, thorough, and reassuring - we never once felt in the dark.

Most importantly, David delivered. He navigated the legal complexities efficiently and thoughtfully and took our adoption case over the finish line when we weren’t sure that would be possible due to a judge who seemed hell-bent on making it as difficult as possible. Because of his expertise and dedication, our family is now complete.

If you are looking for an attorney who is knowledgeable, responsive, compassionate, and truly committed to his clients, David is the one. We are endlessly grateful for his work and would recommend him without hesitation to anyone navigating adoption or family law.
reviews.ownerResponse: It was our pleasure to represent this wonderful, loving family and we were honored to be a part of their adoption story.  We all felt that we were members of their family as well.

My son was also harassed, threatened, and continuously beat down mentally and on occasion, physically. My son is a completely different kid now. reviews.text:
Edit: they are correct when they say they did not personally represent me but they were the lawyer representing our estate. They are lying when they say they have never met me because I met the lawyer at the hearing. So that should tell you enough about the law firm. They lied in their response... not someone I would want to represent me. I’m not upset about what he states the judge decided, yet my stepfather, his client said that’s what the lawyer told him to do. All I’ve asked for is a response and the documents that refer to our estate. If I was never someone they have dealt with then why would he put my name in documents? Don’t take their response as an okay. I am not a scorned individual.  I am trying to make sure you don’t get treated the same way.

Absolutely worst experience. They will lie to you constantly saying he’s in a hearing but will call back, 1 minute after, if he doesn’t like what a client says. The man said I was dense and must not be able to read. He does not respond in a timely matter and takes forever with a case.

His office staff seem to hate life. It may be a personal experience but someone who talks down on a client who has no knowledge of estates doesn’t deserve your money. If he felt he had the right to speak to me in that manner who’s to say it won’t be you. It will most likely be a stressed you that’s probably dealing with a death and the estate of the person who passed.

You do not want the extra stress this man will give you. There are much better lawyers out there and no one deserves to be spoken to in that manner. I promise you will be better off elsewhere.

My relationship with the firm became my lifeline. The entire staff always had my back, kept me informed and prepared during the process. Trusting their experience and turning everything over to them was EXACTLY what I needed to care for myself and my husband. In conclusion, my case was resolved in a timely fashion and I was awarded $500,000.00. Much more than my expectation. reviews.text:
Ryan Zapalac has been an invaluable ally in my legal journey, offering not just expert legal representation but also honest and transparent guidance. From the beginning, Ryan gave me a fair estimate of his fees and assured me that if he didn’t use the full amount of the retainer, he would refund the difference—demonstrating his commitment to fairness and integrity. His upfront approach and clear communication about costs set him apart, providing me with peace of mind and confidence that I was in good hands.

Ryan consistently gives the best advice, and what truly sets him apart is his willingness to admit when he doesn’t have all the answers. He isn’t afraid to say, “I don’t know,” and is quick to refer you to other attorneys if it means getting you the help you need. This honesty and humility are rare qualities in the legal world and show his genuine commitment to his clients’ best interests.

Beyond his exceptional legal skills, Ryan brings a fascinating and diverse background to the table, including fluency in multiple languages, which reflects his broad perspective and unique approach to his practice. Not only is he an outstanding attorney, but he also takes the time to understand his clients' needs on a personal level, offering support and insight that extends beyond the courtroom.

Ryan’s calm demeanor and strategic thinking made me feel secure throughout my case, and his vast knowledge and skill set proved to be crucial in navigating the complexities of the legal process. If you are looking for a lawyer who combines expertise, transparency, and an impressive personal background, Ryan Zapalac is the attorney you want by your side. His commitment to his clients and his genuine, approachable nature make him truly exceptional.

Thank you, Ryan, for your guidance, honesty, and dedication. Your work has made a significant impact, and I am grateful to have had you in my corner.

reviews.text:
My grandfather had mineral rights to some land we had owned in Shiner Texas.  When he died, everything went to my grandmother.  By the time she wrote her new will, everyone had forgotten about the Shiner mineral rights.  Long story short, by the time my grandmother passed, a lot of people had died and nothing was logged about the mineral rights.  I am the last one alive from our family and ownership of the rights was a bit confusing.  From where I stand (inside the family) it was quite straight forward, but legally, it was anything but.

SimpleLaw got it all straightened out.  Thank you, SimpleLaw.
reviews.ownerResponse: James thank you for sharing this and for trusting SimpleLaw with something that had clearly become layered and complicated over time. He tried telling me to get a lawyer when I knew all I needed is the date of the procedure so I could prove I wasn’t even in their town the time of. June 3rd of 2022 while driving a freight train, I slammed into a loaded tractor trailer that was parked on the track in Houston Texas. After the incident I had to get some work done on my lower back due to the hard impact. I missed a total of 93 days of work. While getting therapy & was really worried about how I was going to pay my bills while being off during therapy & rehabilitation. I contacted McGuire Injury Law & just from the phone conversation I was able to breathe a sigh of relief. Jeff McGuire which is the attorney I spoke with was fabulous. Over a series of phone calls as well as meetings he worked with me by pointing me in the right direction as far as which doctors could help me best. The law firm offered financial support while I was off & it was offered at a very reasonable repay rate if I needed it. I wasn't expecting to get a whole lot of money. All I was really concerned with was getting my lost wages as well as my medical bills paid. I spent a total of 93 days in and out of different doctors offices and rehabilitation centers that worked on my lower back and when all was said done Jeff McGuire contacted the trucking companies insurance and explained to them all that had happened as well as what I was out financially and low and behold the trucking company totally agreed with the amount we requested. After medical bills and attorneys fees I put some money in my pocket pay all medical bills and go back to work. Jeff was great. Very informative as well as understanding about my concerns. If you or anyone you know is in need of a personal injury lawyer, I highly highly highly recommend McGuire Injury Law.

My life changed when I met McGuire injury law firm unfortunately it was because I had been injured in a job setting. My family and I were in fear of how we would be able to survive. I first heard about Clint when I was working with his father on the Railroad in 2009. I was told that he was a better Attorney than Steve Young, who at the time was the Top FELA Railroad Attorney. Forward to August 2018 where I was involved in a Limo accident while on duty. A couple of days later the injury got worse and I went and saw a doctor, who advised to go see a back specialist. I was placed on injury by the company and I was advised to contact an Attorney to protect myself for having a on the job injury. I got Clint's office number and called. Clint took my call and gave me good advice on what to be prepared for and how to handle to complications that come from being injured. I found Clint to be very well versed in FELA cases and the National Agreements, Clint advised that I was eligible for OTVA payments and helped me get them when the Union didn't know how to apply for them and the company played dumb. It finally took a threat of filing suit to get what was contractually owed to me.
Clint was there every step of the way, (as was his Staff) he always answered my telephone calls, emails and texts. He would check in on me and kept me informed of the status of everything going on in my case. When it came time for depositions, Clint prepped me for my testimony and I was more prepared than I have ever been to testify.  After almost (3) years, (3) Major surgeries and (8) injections we went to mediation and much to my satisfaction we settled.
Clint is honest, truthful and has great integrity, he is fearless when it comes to taking on big Corporations and protecting his Clients.
I am proud to call him my Friend.

Michael Jauch

did little to nothing for my case. literally just showed up for couple zoom meetings, “drafted” a couple of things. which the opposing party drew up. sent a few emails. boom 5000 dollar retainer gone. i did all the mediating with the other party. i know im paying for representation but damn. i definitely got ripped off. my lawyer left the firm in the middle of my case and no one bothered to tell me. these people are still asking me for more money. very disappointed.

I recently got a divorce because my wife was unfaithful to me for MANY years. Not only did i have pictures and text to prove it but she put me in debt. I worked my whole life providing for my family and i hired Scott brown & associates to help with this divorce and i regret it 100%. The time it took to get this done was about a year,  not only did they switch 3 different lawyers to my case, they switched my lawyer the weekend before my mediation and Megan new absolutely NOTHING about my case when it came to mediation. They charged me every Nickle and penny I had and didn't provide ANY of the documentation that i provided each lawyer  when it came to mediation. I would not recommend this firm to ANYONE. ABSOLUTELY TERRIBLE. Took weeks to every get an answer on anything either. Communication was VERY poor. Never heard any update throughout my case.

AVOID AT ALL COST. they get you with low cost initial consultation, but make sure to ask their hourly fee. Paula is NOT your lawyer. Hired her to keep child support. she actually managed to cancel it for me saying "you dont really need child support". Then they just started sending me invoices without explanations and why it is going to 10K. She did not do anything we talked about and only made it worse. Imagine going to a doctor with your health problem, doctor telling you he will take care of it, and then they just start charging you bunch of money without explaining what for, and without any damn results. Lawyers should be accountable for what they say and promise, just like everybody else. STAY AWAY. WASTE OF TIME, MONEY AND A LOT OF REGRET.

one star is too high for William A. Brown.

We had him write our wills in 2015 and he had refused to included EXECUTORS and SELF PROVING AFFIDAVIT in our wills that are required by Texas Estate Law.  We were suspicious about his intention refusing to include the critical requirements in wills written in Texas.  If a will does not include Executor and Self Proving Affidavit, it cannot be probated because not having two critical requirements in the will, it is not a valid will.  If possible in anyway, it would cost so much money to probate.  I cannot imagine any honest estate lawyer would write wills without Executors and Self Proving Affidavit and refuses to include them in wills despite the clients requests.

When he refused to meet our request to include the two item (Executors and Self Proving Affidavit) in our wills, we told him that we did not need his service and asked to refund 50% of the fee we paid upfront ($800.00) because he was the husband of my acquaintance.  I told him that he had two weeks to refund the money but he did not send the check.

When I wrote him an email that I was going to file a complaint against him to Houston Bar Association, he wrote me back, "The check is in the mail."   We received the 50% of the fee I paid ($400.00).

Because we were dealing with a lawyer, we kept the copies of all email exchanges with William Brown, the copies of draft wills he had prepared, and the copy of the check he refunded.  They are in our bank safety deposit box and they will be remained there.

If anyone is going to have any estate lawyer to write your will, study the requirements in wills written in Texas by going on web site provided by Texas Young Lawyers before you go to see a lawyer.  It is a very helpful website regarding Texas estate law.

I would never recommend William Brown to anyone need to write a will.

From the moment we called the office, Agnes made us feel welcomed and genuinely cared for. She was incredibly friendly, professional, and most importantly — knowledgeable. Agnes handled all of our paperwork with care and promptly presented everything to Mr. Whitehead. Within just 2–3 days, she followed up with us and had already started processing our case. reviews.text: We had struggled to get a newly turned 18 years old incapacitated son the Medicaid we thought he was entitled to have. Upon turning 18, his adoption Medicaid was terminated and he was placed on Medicaid through SSI. Shortly thereafter, SS called and said that he should be on DAC SS and they changed it. We thought "okay, no big deal" but it was because several weeks later we received a Medicaid cancellation notice because he was no longer on SSI. Repeated trips to local Medicaid offices yielded more forms to fill out: "try this one" we heard more than once. Enter the knowledgeable and magnanimous Les Berry. At the consultation meeting with him he assembled all the facts and stated that our son was indeed eligible for Medicaid, without trying to sell his services. I immediately shifted from consultation to engagement and received Medicaid in about a month. Thank you Les.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for sharing your experience! We know how frustrating and confusing the Medicaid process can be, especially when it involves navigating multiple agencies and eligibility changes. We’re so glad Les was able to step in, provide clarity, and get your son the benefits he deserves. Your story is exactly why we do what we do — to help families find peace of mind through knowledgeable and compassionate guidance. We truly appreciate your trust in Your Legacy Legal Care® and are so happy to have been part of your journey!

reviews.text:
We had recently moved from a different state and wanted to update our trust according to Texas law. Our previous lawyer informed us we would just need a simple amendment and it shouldn’t cost us very much. After meeting with an assistant at this company (not an actual lawyer), we were told they’d have to redo our entire trust to the tune of $5000-$8990 depending on which package we wanted. Red flags went up so we decided to contact our previous lawyer who suggested we contact the Texas State bar and shop around for a different company. Thank goodness we did! Our previous lawyer was correct! In fact, our trust was even more secure from our previous state that we would’ve put ourselves in jeopardy had we followed the advice of this company. For a fraction of the cost, we were able to do a simple amendment and secure our trust here in Texas. This company is very expensive, and inaccurate. Shop around, don’t take their word as gospel truth! I would’ve given one star, but instead gave 2 because the assistant was kind and only doing her job.

UPDATE: Thanks for your reply to my review! I can see how some people may require what you stated if they lacked a sound trust to start with, however, had someone from your team looked up a simple law from our previous state, you’d realize our trust was iron clad compared to Texas law and again we would’ve been charged thousands of dollars unnecessarily. Do not be fooled! Wills and trusts are protected by the constitution and are recognized regardless of which state you move from and to. Do your homework! This company is extremely expensive and inaccurate when it came to our case. A simple check of our previous state law would have confirmed this. It’s also not accurate that you need an entire trust redone - that only benefits this company’s pocket, not the consumer.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We’re sorry to hear that our approach didn’t align with your expectations but are happy that you found someone to do what you were looking for. Every family’s situation is unique, and our recommendations are based on Texas law and the comprehensive planning our clients have come to rely on. Our goal is to provide every option to clients so they can make an informed decision about what is best for them.

At our firm, we do not amend estate plans drafted by other attorneys. This is a deliberate decision made to avoid potential legal and liability issues especially since most plans do not meet our standards. Instead, we offer full restatements that allow us to ensure your plan is not only legally sound in Texas, but also aligned with what most of our clients are truly looking for: peace of mind that their loved ones will be taken care of, their assets protected, and court involvement avoided.

Many of our clients come to us because they want to plan for the possibility of long-term care needs, including protecting assets while qualifying for benefits like Medicaid. Others want to ensure their families can avoid costly and time-consuming court processes such as probate and guardianship. In our experience, these goals cannot be reliably accomplished through a simple amendment—especially when the laws vary so much from state to state.

While we understand our services may not be the right fit for everyone, we are committed to providing clear guidance and high-quality legal solutions that go beyond “bare minimum” planning. We appreciate your kind words about our team member and wish you the very best moving forward.

If you’re reading this review and want to ensure your plan truly protects your family, your future, and your legacy—we invite you to schedule a strategy session with our team to learn what your options are. We’re here to help!

reviews.text: My sister and I met with GP Schoemakers about a week ago in regard to land that is in our mother's  name. reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you.

reviews.text:
My brother and I met with Gratia on 03/03/2025 for legal counsel about some land that our mother (93) has, which we were trying to put in our name. We came with a Will and the Power of Attorney. Durning our visit, we were informed that the POA that we obtained in 2016 was only valid for 5 years and that the laws for property has since changed. We also learned that the Will was not specific in the description of the property in question. She instructed us on what steps to take next and how to go about it.
This was our very first encounter with her, and first impressions say a lot. We look forward to doing business with her in the future.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for your review. It is important to be aware that Texas Law changes periodically. Also, most people are unaware but Texas Law allows for POA Rejection with "Good Faith" Reasoning.
Under Texas Estates Code § 751.201 and § 751.206, banks and other financial institutions can refuse a POA if they have a good faith reason.
Some banks use the "staleness" of a POA (older than 5 years) as their justification for refusal. This can severely impact some ones estate plan.
Which makes it so important to review estate planning documents every 3-5 years.
Additionally, In Texas, as of September 1, 2023, a new requirement exists for using a power of attorney (POA) to transfer real property:
The New Requirement states: POA Must Be Recorded Before Use in Real Estate Transactions.

reviews.text:
I was rear-ended at high speed and ended up with a herniated disc in my lower back, a torn rotator cuff, and a concussion that left me dealing with migraines for months. I couldn’t sit for long, couldn’t lift my arm properly, and had to miss weeks of work. The insurance company tried to downplay everything like it was “just soreness.”

That’s when I hired The Patoli Law Firm. Ahsan immediately took control of the case. reviews.text:
When you need a lawyer, you hire THE BEST, and Tad Nelson & Associates are absolutely that — no question about it!!!

Over Christmas, my entire life was turned upside down by false allegations, which led to misdemeanor charges and a protective order. I was scared, overwhelmed, and honestly didn’t know how I was going to get through it. I called Tad Nelson & Associates Christmas Eve.

From the very beginning, Tad went above and beyond. He gave me his personal cell phone number and answered every call and every text, even during the holidays and on weekends. I never once felt ignored or brushed off — I felt like I mattered.

That Saturday, I met with Tad to complete paperwork. He told me that he and his team would do everything they could to get me home by New Year’s Eve so I could see my family.

On Monday morning, Tad and Amber were already at the courthouse by 8:00 AM, fighting hard to have the charges dropped.

By 1:00 PM that same day, Tad called me and told me my charges were COMPLETELY DISMISSED. I collapsed. I dropped to the ground crying in disbelief. One phone call changed everything — fear, panic, and uncertainty turned into overwhelming relief.

But they weren’t done yet. The false protective order was still keeping me from my family. Tad and Amber refused to stop. Amber fought relentlessly, pushing the court again and again so I could go home.

On New Year’s Eve at 6:30 PM, both Tad and Amber personally called me and told me the words I will never forget: “The orders are signed! You can go home!”

Without Tad Nelson and Amber, I would have been separated from my family for 90 days. Because of them, it took less than 7. They didn’t just do their jobs — they fought for me, believed in me, and gave me my life back.

The love, respect, dedication, and heart that Tad and Amber put into their work is beyond words. Tad and Amber, I know you’re reading this — and I need you to know that I am forever grateful. Thank you for fighting for me when I couldn’t fight for myself. Thank you for bringing me home.

If you are facing criminal charges and feel scared, lost, or alone — call Tad Nelson & Associates. Period.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing such a heartfelt and powerful review. It truly means more than words can express. From the moment you reached out, we knew how urgent and personal this situation was, and our entire focus was on protecting you and getting you back where you belonged, with your family.

Cases like yours remind us why we do this work. When someone is facing false allegations and the system moves too quickly or unfairly, it’s our job to step in, push back, and make sure the truth is heard. Helping you reunite with your child and your family was exactly the outcome we were fighting for, and we’re grateful we were able to do that.

Thank you for trusting us during one of the most difficult moments of your life. We’re honored to have stood beside you, and we wish you nothing but peace and strength moving forward.
- Tad & Amber

reviews.text: Your a lawyer, still part of the system to suppress. Start thinking outside the system and you will do more good that way.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I believe this review is connected to a jury verdict that was returned yesterday in a very serious and emotionally difficult case. I cannot say that with absolute certainty, but given the timing and the nature of the comment, it is a reasonable assumption and my response is written with that understanding in mind.

Cases like this are never easy. When a life has been lost, there is no outcome that truly feels like justice to everyone involved. I fully recognize and deeply respect that the victim’s family is hurting. Their grief is real, and it is something no courtroom result can ever erase. They are in my thoughts and prayers.

As a criminal defense lawyer, my role is not to minimize loss, excuse harm, or ignore pain. My role is to stand between the power of the government and an individual citizen and ensure that the process is fair, lawful, and just. That responsibility exists even when the case is emotionally charged and the stakes are extraordinarily high.

I take that duty seriously. I work hard. I work honestly. And I work relentlessly to ensure that every client receives a defense grounded in the facts, the law, and the Constitution. In this case, my partner Amber and I prepared thoroughly, presented the evidence carefully, and advocated for our client with integrity and respect for the gravity of the situation.

It is important for people reading this to understand a fundamental truth about the justice system: criminal defense attorneys do not decide outcomes. Juries do. Our role is to make sure the jury hears all the evidence and that the law is followed. When a verdict is returned, it reflects the collective judgment of citizens who were tasked with weighing the facts—not indifference to loss or suffering.

I understand that some people will never agree with the work criminal defense lawyers do, no matter how carefully, ethically, or responsibly it is done. That reaction often comes from deep pain, and I accept that as part of this profession.

I could ask for this review to be removed, but I won’t. I believe it is important for the public to see the reality of criminal defense work and the emotional weight that comes with it. One side may feel relief, while another feels anger or grief; and both realities can exist at the same time.

To anyone considering my firm: know that when I take on a case, I do so with seriousness, honesty, and respect for every person involved. I will always work as hard as the law allows, I will always tell the truth, and I will always fight for my client within the bounds of ethics and justice.

And to those who are hurting: I am truly sorry for your pain. I do not take lightly the human cost behind any case. You are in my thoughts and prayers.

— Tad Nelson

reviews.text: Mr. Nelson was probably too busy to provide an adequate defense of a Class C misdemeanor or to provide adequate feedback or updates on the status of the case.  After he basically informed us to not contact his office as frequently and that he would provide the necessary information when available, we discharged him and hired another attorney.  Our case has been open in Texas City Municipal Court since August, 2025 with no end in sight.  Very frustrating!  Oh, and he required a non-refundable fee prior to accepting the case.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Yep, I am so sorry we let you down. This case was a class C. That means a traffic ticket handled in the municipal courts.I hadn't set the case for trial yet becauser I had reached out to the prosecutor and was assured he was going to talk with the witness. I thought once he talked with the witness the case would be dismissed. The next setting the case would have been set on the trial docket. Unfortunatley, I did not go fast enough for this client and I realize my relationship with Robert Barfield, the prosecutor was not as good as I thought. As far as money, you signed a contract written by the State Bar and there is actually a paragraph about return of unearned retainer. Anywho, I am sorry we let you down and totally wish you the best. I also forwarded your review to the prosecutor to let him know the wedge he helped create between me and my cliuent. Even after being fired I am still working to get this case dismissed.

Good luck. So sorry, Tad

reviews.text: Dear tad nelson and associates, i wanted to leave this review on behalf of John Sullivan. I just wanted to praise you and your team on a job well done, you faught for him the entire time and always kept pushing for his best outcome. Thank you very much for all of y’all’s amazing help.

reviews.text: The guy is well known around the courthouse and by the judges, likely because he is quite good at what he does. As I understand it, him and his team are very well qualified and extremely experienced/knowledgeable to provide you with a well built defense. The only thing I will say is at times It’s difficult to get in contact with them and or have them return phone calls. I feel like communication could very much be improved, but some of the blame I put on myself for that though, and also cause I had two separate cases I hired him for, and you have to remember you aren’t their only client. At the end of the day, I could be in a lot more trouble but I’m not, because of him, which when you boil it down is what counts. A good legal defense isn’t cheap, but If you don’t want to be in jail, call this man. He often offers discounts for paying in full up front or cash, so he will work with you. If you have concerns or feel like you’re out of the loop, call his office and tell them that.

reviews.text:
****Please stay away from this company for any of your legal matters they will rip you off*******I had a disappointing and unethical experience working with this firm. In my case, I found the communication unclear and the overall process lacked transparency. I also experienced unexpected fees through a referral arrangement, which I wish had been better explained upfront.

Based on my experience, I would strongly recommend anyone engaging with them to ask detailed questions about the fee structure, referral terms, and expected timelines before signing any agreement. I with i could have handled the matter directly with my insurance provider, which might have turned out to be a smoother experience. AVOID this company as much as possible.
----------------------------
Below is my response to what this firm says they are tranparent and for this Fact they have i have never recieved a response.

Below email sent which i never got a response for ---

If your intent is truly righteous, then please respond directly to the points I’ve raised below.
I stand by every word I’ve said and will continue to do so.

As stated in your own agreement:

“Forty percent (40%) of any settlement proceeds, court awarded proceeds, or any payments made after the commencement of representation… (attorney is owed nothing if unable to obtain settlement proceeds or court awarded proceeds on behalf of client).”

This clearly means the 40% contingency applies only to proceeds obtained after your representation began. The portion covering repairs I completed before your involvement — specifically the tree removal ($7700) and fence repair ($6000) — does not fall under that definition

Another email which i never got a response yet.----

Can you please clarify where in the documentation it shows that these items fall under my deductible? Based on your disbursement sheet, it appears to be a flat calculation, while my deductible is $7,600, not $13,700. The additional amount represents out-of-pocket expenses I personally covered before your firm’s involvement.

You’ve stated that these amounts were removed from the fee calculation, yet no supporting figures or breakdown have been provided. Please share documentation reflecting where and how the $13,700 was excluded.

I am not seeking any favor or additional payment — I’m simply asking for a transparent and factual reconciliation based on our agreement. Thus far, no data from your side has addressed these points directly.
---------------------------
If you don't respect your terms then yes i have right to go to Texas Bar or who so ever can make you understand above clause.
---------------------------
reviews.ownerResponse: The terms of the Agreement were simple and in plain English.  We are also one of the few firms that will reduce our contingency fee up front if the insured has a contract with a public adjuster.  Mr. Yadav was aware of this and agreed to it.  I will also say that he has been in breach of this agreement by not providing a forwarding address when he went back to India.  He has refused until we include a bigger payout for him than what he contractually agreed to. He has also threatened to file a complaint with the Texas Bar unless we comply with his demands.  Just another person who wants to manipulate after the fact and post bad reviews to pressure others to get what they want.  I can't negotiate with terrorists, I ethically do not believe that is the right thing to do.

reviews.text: I have worked with this firm for years when it comes to getting my clients paid for their insurance claim.  I cannot recommend any attorney firm more than Chad T. They keep the client informed of the process, and typically have a very positive out come.  Its is best to have a firm that has a group of attorneys with a ton of experience and an office staff that are state of the art. I have used other firms and will tell you, they will simplify this part of the process.  I would recommend them to all insureds facing the unreasonable denial of an insurance carrier

My wife and I hired The Chad T. Wilson Law Firm to handle our hail damage dispute with our insurance company.  Our assigned attorney was Amanda Fulton and her team.  We had never gone through a process like this with an insurance company and not sure  what to expect.  Amanda was very good at explaining the steps and what we could expect throughout the process.  Our case had to go to mediation prior to going to trial and once again Amanda and team made the mediation process very easy.  They explained the process and what role we would play.  Amanda navigated the mediation with skill and completely professional.  Amanda successfully argued our case and we received a very good outcome from the mediation and settled our case with our insurance company to our satisfaction.  I would highly recommend using Amanda and her team.

Recently used Donald Green at Chad Wilson for a insurance roof claim. Very pleased. Insurance had been stonewalling me for 6 months and I finally just got exasperated. Contacted Chad Wilson who took the case. The best part was I did not have to deal with claims at all after they took the case. After around 5 months, insurance paid claim in full. I'll also add that Donald was very candid with how the process worked. Things like how long it could take, the range of outcomes, etc.
Amanda Fulton was wonderful to work with!  She explained everything along the way and made the process simple.

Her persistence and clear communication provided us with confidence and peace of mind throughout the process. I highly recommend this firm to anyone seeking a legal team that genuinely cares about their clients and delivers outstanding results.

Believe these reviews staff is rude , paralegal Ericka always has an attitude hates her job or helping people out either or . Don’t go here for divorce cases keep looking !

I’ve heard good things about the attorneys at this law firm, but unfortunately, my experience with their paralegal Ericka Rodriguez was disappointing. As someone who has worked in law enforcement for over 10 years, I’ve interacted with many legal professionals, and I’ve never come across a paralegal as rude and dismissive as the one I spoke to here.
Not only did she offer no helpful advice, but she also hung up on me mid-conversation and didn’t even have the courtesy to call back. That kind of unprofessional behavior speaks volumes. It’s unfortunate because I was seriously considering hiring this firm for my divorce case. I'm now glad I chose to go elsewhere.
While the lawyers may be competent in handling smaller matters as some reviews suggest the front line experience matters just as much. And in my case, it was enough to walk away.
I hired Lozano Law office for 3 cases, I cannot thank them enough for their support. If you want your case resolved, consider hiring this firm.

I am extremely grateful for the exceptional care and professionalism Sebastian and Ericka demonstrated while handling my case. From the initial consultation to the final resolution, they were attentive, communicative, and genuinely invested in achieving the best possible outcome. Their expertise and dedication made a significant difference during a challenging time. I highly recommend their services to anyone seeking knowledgeable and compassionate legal representation.
Lozano Law was knowledgeable, responsive, and truly dedicated to achieving the best possible outcome- I highly recommend their services!!
Sebastian and Ericka handled my case with care and I am grateful for their services.

Unfortunately, my experience with Sebastian Figueroa and Ericka Rodriguez during my divorce case was extremely disappointing.

I felt unsupported throughout the process, and it seemed like their main priority was getting paid through MetLife rather than truly helping me. Communication was disorganized, and it felt like they were juggling more clients than they could handle effectively.

If you’re going through something as serious and personal as a divorce, I strongly recommend reaching out to Denise Williams Lewis instead. She is professional, compassionate, and truly advocates for her clients. Denise handled my case with the care and support I needed during one of the most difficult times in my life.

I was very disappointed with my recent experience with this law firm specifically with the behavior and knowledge of the paralegal staff specifically ERICKA RODRIGUEZ ! Paralegals play a crucial role in guiding clients through the legal process, yet the one I interacted with was unhelpful, dismissive, and at times, outright rude.
I had simple, reasonable questions about what to expect during the court process, and instead of providing clear guidance, I was met with a condescending attitude and vague, uninformative answers. Her lack of professionalism and knowledge made it difficult for me to feel confident in the firm overall and even made me reconsider hiring the attorney.
If this firm wants to maintain its reputation, I strongly suggest they invest in hiring professional, well trained, and courteous paralegal who understand that part of their job is client support not acting as a barrier to it.

We can NOT thank the Lozano law office enough for representing us in our adoption case!!! What normally takes months to years they were able to get it done effectively within 3 months!!!! Thank you again Erika and Sebatian!!!!!

From Frances answering the phone when I first called to Ericka Rodriguez helping me get through the process, Lozano Law firm was a top notch experience. Ericka was so thorough with my case. She took the time to understand my needs, and provide me with a solution that truly worked for me. I'm so grateful for this law firm, and especially the way everything was handled.

I enlisted this firm's assistance in early May. They took their sweet time contacting me. After [supposedly] engaging the opposing party with a simple demand letter (a copy of which I've never seen), they, without warning, they walked away from my case with ZERO explanation as to why. They further provided ZERO information as to what -- if any -- correspondence had been received from the opposing party to date, leaving me with no choice but to hire another firm to represent my interests. Do not use this firm.

The law office reached out to me and requested information multiple times so they could build a case, but once I submitted everything the communication stopped. There were no updates until they told me their work was completed. I'm not sure what they did, as they became non-responsive but it was a waste of time and money.
Paralegal is not very knowledgeable and unable to clearly and effectively communicate. The attorneys are absent and never heard from. Would not recommend.
Ilda Longoria and this law firm are very unprofessional. They are not what I expected when I allowed them to represent me. I wouldn't recommend them to nobody.

I recently contacted Lozano Law Office, P.C. for legal representation, and so far, my experience has been very positive. I expressed the assistance I needed, and they took the time to listen, address my concerns, and provide clear answers to my questions. Their professionalism and attentiveness have been reassuring. I will update my review in the future once the case progresses and everything has been addressed. I look forward to sharing my updated experience.

Zero stars This att. Sebastian works for lozano law and I can honestly say if you want to lose your case and your money and get lied too and not do anything to help your case yes go to them, I don’t know about anyone else but this character named Sebastian shame on you.

I give lawyer Claudia Lozano 5 stars, she is an excellent human being and very professional, unfortunately she is surrounded by unprofessional people, especially the new receptionist who is very arrogant and rude.

I would have preferred to speak with the lead attorney, but instead they assigned me another one I hadn't requested. I had to call repeatedly for information, and in the end, I had to go to court and lose a day of work. They essentially did the same thing I could have done by going in person, and in the end, they still made me lose a day of work. Very poor service.

I would not recommend this law firm for any civil related matters such as boundary disputes.  I signed a contract for representation via MetLaw
They were trifling inasmuch as communications between them and MetLaw, they took the case for a boundary dispute, then, unexpectedly resigned from the case without any logical explanation or my knowledge.  Then, sent me a letter,  stating " as you are aware".  In my opinion,  if you sign a contract with a client, then withdraw without just cause or communication, your conduct is unethical. What they are doing is probably trying to take too many cases, being greedy, while sacrificing ethics harming the client. They are totally unprofessional and in my opinion lazy. Furthermore,  they should resign from their membership at MetLaw, because they are making MetLaw look bad from their unprofessional conduct.

I am deeply disappointed in the lack of professionalism exhibited by this law firm. Despite our consistent efforts to address a lease issue, we have struggled to receive adequate follow-up. It is concerning that I have only been able to speak with my lead attorney once, and have had sporadic conversations with the paralegal. Additionally, numerous emails have gone unanswered, leaving us at a significant disadvantage in our legal dispute. The absence of proper professional guidance has hindered our ability to navigate this situation effectively.

I would advise anyone facing a similar situation, especially those with smaller claims, to consider seeking representation from an agency that can dedicate the necessary time and attention to their case.

She has several business profiles to cover her tracks, but she is not only expensive, she is also unapologetically rude, and will leave you hanging in the worst time of the fight and prob acuse you of the same thing you are defending yourself from as an excuse. Worst hire ever, trust this review and skip her and you will be much better off. Just leaving this review to help someone else.
reviews.ownerResponse:
This review is misleading and leaves out many important details. That this client came to us with a highly complex case, then repeatedly ignored legal advice, made choices that directly harmed his own position, and became hostile and disrespectful toward staff. Despite multiple warnings, he continued to act against his own best interests and created further problems.

Our office will not tolerate combative and disrespectful behavior or allow a client to sabotage their own case and then attempt to shift blame.

Unfortunately, no amount of skill or effort can overcome a client’s refusal to cooperate or follow legal advice.

reviews.text: Stacy Valdez is the best.. no bs and straight to the point doesn’t mess around. I actually had the opportunity to watch her in a court room it she was masterful.. She represented me years ago and I will never forget her or her law firm
I had a good experience with this law firm. Stacy and her staff were very responsive and fair priced.
The best family law attorney in Texas, hands down. Stacey doesn't just work for you, she wins for you.

Stacey really helped me out and made me feel reassured in a stressful case. She fought for me to the fullest extent, and was on my side every step of the way. I highly recommend Stacey for anybody dealing with a family matter.

This is the attorney you want on your case especially if there is an amicus involved. What should’ve been an easy win for us, turned south because the amicus was pitiful and delayed our case a good year. We hired Stacey and suddenly the amicus started acting right. Her office fixed several issues that the previous lawyers office straight up neglected and we were able to turn our case around. I was very ready to see her eat up the opposing council but because Stacey has a reputation like no other, they ran scared the day of trial and asked for a second mediation. Enter Michaela. She is calm cool and collected and KNOWS HER STUFF! We walked out with everything we asked for. I am extremely grateful for this entire office. Shoutout to Danielle for taking EVERY call and email and always keeping me in the loop.

How you conduct yourself in a public setting, especially when it comes to the way you treat the staff (which was boarder line abuse considering they were following policy)  is how you will conduct yourself in your place of business or dealing with clients.
reviews.ownerResponse: Lisa Hunter has never been associated with my office, in any manner. I believe this person is leaving a low star rating on behalf of another for reasons completely unrelated to my practice of law.

reviews.text: There is a difference between being a shark of a lawyer and a bully. This woman is a bully. If you are looking for a lawyer with any sort of compassion, this is not the one. Especially not if you are ill or have a mental disability. She represented my mother, not myself

reviews.text: Stacey Valdez and her team are brilliant. They will fight for you, and let you know you have the absolute best team in your corner. I couldn't have asked for better representation, and they truly give you confidence and comfort in your situation!

reviews.text: I don’t know you either. I Left my gmail logged on a public computer and someone used my account to give you a 1 star. Here I changed it to a 5 to help balance off these fake reviews. Good luck on building your business.

This person has no connection, whatsoever, with my firm.

This person has never been a client at my firm.

reviews.text:
While this firm has not represented me, I do have dealings with them. This attorney has represented my former spouse during our child support proceedings and a spurious case from the same former spouse that was recently dismissed after over 3 years for want of prosecution due to a REFUSAL to serve the court papers to me. Additionally, during every time I have spoken with them, I have only been met with open hostility for no reason that I have been informed of. One such example of this was Valdez's statement in court (during our first ever interaction) where she threatened to attack me (not specified how or if it was physical or verbal), depending on what was said next. My rating of 1-star is due to the absolute lack of professionalism I have always encountered from them. If even one of my interactions with this office, other than noted below, was professional and had some form of semi-courteous exchange, I would never have even written this rating/review.

On a side note, there was one paralegal there that did try to help get the court documents and get me served in the spurious case, but she was shot down by the higher ups.
reviews.ownerResponse: I did not represent this person for any legal matter; indeed, I do not even know who he is.

Gave known Greg for fourty years   Knew he was great at what he did but asked for advice about matter I was handling and without skipping a beat pointed me in the right direction    Highly recommend

I was recommended to Mr. Greg Enos by another lawyer. The first visit went great. Mr. Enos was friendly and to the point even complimented me, Then things quickly took a turn for the worst. He turned my case over to other lawyers in the firm who never ever go into the office. I could never get anyone on the phone when I needed something. I follow their direction and not long into it  I received the letter saying they were not a “good fit” for my case.  This was after that they took my thousands of dollars and did not refund anything. They did nothing for me. They served my husband once and I had to direct the server day by day until he was served. All the information that they got on my case was of my doing, my digging and my. deliverance to them. I would call the office to speak to someone they were so rude and not understanding at all. Mr. Greg insulted me by telling me “ you’re the stupidest woman I know“ . At the end of that conversation, he was going to get in touch with me that was in March. We are now in July. That’s the kind of service. I got throughout this whole ordeal. I am so disappointed. It leaves me with no lawyer and no money to get another one right now. I highly recommend that you stay away from this Law Firm.. they are the epitome of what the general public thinks of lawyers and attorneys. I’m sure there are some good ones out there but now I’m afraid to talk to anyone. As far as I’m concerned, they are thieves.

After being served, Enos Family Law was a godsend. It's fair to say that nothing makes your life more and causes more stress than family court and custody battles. I thought mine was going to be about 100 times harder than it was. All thanks to Greg, Catherine and Stephanie.

I feel very fortunate to have chosen the Enos Family Law Firm, and if I need legal advice in the future, I won’t hesitate to return to them. I highly recommend this firm to anyone in need of legal assistance – they are professional, friendly, and effective!

Greg and Paul were very thorough, super supportive and professional during my divorce process and they became dear friends to me in a time when I truly needed it. Placing my trust in them was the best thing that I could have done and I will forever be thankful to them.
To the team at The Enos Law Firm, you are not just another case, but someone going through a tough time, and they assure you of their commitment and empathy.

reviews.text: Best family law attorney around.  It is extremely difficult for a man to find a good divorce and custody attorney in the State of Texas, and the Enos Law Firm knocked it out of the park for me.   I recommend this law firm to anyone that has custody issues or is going through a divorce.  This law firm will exceed your expectations!
Best family attorney in Houston. Got me custody of my children in Galveston county. Very tough to do. He did it as promised.

I was represented Pro Bono by this law firm. I quickly realized the lack of care within their representation. I was lied to and manipulated into signing documents in order to keep my case at low cost. When I called to question the process, I was ignored. I called back several times and finally heard back from Mr. Enos. I broke down in tears asking for answers. Mr. Enos replied, "Take your crazy self somewhere else."
He used personal information to insult me when I questioned his firm. He is fully aware that I was diagnosed with PTSD from all of the abuse in my marriage. Not going to court caused irreparable damage to my family as I was never given the chance to speak with a judge about the abuse within my marriage. I was told to sign documents with promises of an eventual court date but the firm stopped returning my calls once I signed the papers. He is probably a good lawyer when he wants to be but for my case he was negligent and corrupt.
Very professional and considerate of what their clients are going through financially and emotionally.

I wish I'd done my divorce myself. They didn't return phone calls and didn't do what they said they would do in a timely manner. Overall they are pleasant to deal with. They said I would be charged a flat fee for my uncontested divorce, but turned around and charged me an extra $1000.  I have heard other attorneys are much worse. Just a rip off. I guess this is par for the course with attorneys. I feel ripped off and just overall betrayed at a time of emotional strain and upheaval in my life.
They work hard for you at a fair price
Divorce is sad and tough. Enos law firm definitely put me at ease. Mr Enos was highly recommended.  I to highly recommend
Excellent family law attorney. Very knowledgeable.

I started the paperwork without a lawyer for a no contest divorce. I wanted a second opinion because my window for error was small since I work abroad. Mr. Enos took my no contest divorce case and assisted me greatly in getting all my paperwork ready and making the necessary changes. He helped me bypass the whole Galveston County friend of the court step to present my case directly to the judge. I'm now a free man. Thank you Mr. Enos.

I'd love to give negatuve 20 stars. I came here to help my mother get a divorce and was told because the divorce was easy id get 400 to 500 dollars back. Also we told them the husband was dangerous and he contacted us and she sent us an email saying she gave him the phone number so we could do it ourselves and it would be cheaper. She also said my mom could get her last name changed. She forgot to tell us it had to be a previous last name. We came up here to complain and they said we never called them, we called several times and was told the lawyer was in a meeting. They said they never sent the husband the email giving our information. They are LIARS AND CROOKS!!! The divorce is final so that's the only good thing that happened. And the sofa was covered in dog hair and my daughter is allergic to dogs. DO NOT USE THIS LAWFIRM!!!!

I called for a consultation about a matter coming up and an appointment was made.  2 hours before our appointment the office called and said he could not see us.  Called back and said he would not see us - no reason given.  It is a Grandparents case so I guess he cannot handle that.

I had a tough child custody/divorce case that I had trusted another lawyer to handle. He let me down. I was recommended to the Enos Law Firm from a family member that had used Christina in the past and had a great experience with them. My case was assigned to Greg Enos, and he was the first lawyer who actually sat with me and walked me through the process. My child was sick at the time with cancer, and he knew as a newly single mom of two, my financial situation was not the greatest. That was the least of his concerns. His office worked with me on payments and he never treated me as lesser than because of it. He helped me get custody of my kids back and guided me through the complicated divorce. He was uplifting and tough, never sugar coated anything but was always supportive. I would recommend him to anyone and everyone going through a tough situation that needs help with custody and divorce. I've never met another lawyer who actually cared more about the client than the paycheck. If you're looking for a family lawyer that will treat you the way you deserve, this is the guy.
